story-context.summary: Story 13.5 implements environment-specific data volumes and transactional seeding with rollback capability for Epic 13: Advanced Seeder Infrastructure. Building on Stories 13.1-13.4 which established the seeder foundation, this story adds NODE_ENV-based data scaling, $transaction() wrapping for atomicity, and rollback safety. The implementation ensures development (50 users), test (5 users), and staging (25 users) environments receive appropriate data volumes with <3s performance in test environment for CI/CD optimization.
story-context.business-value: Enables appropriate data scale per environment: development gets realistic datasets for testing features, test environment gets minimal data for fast CI/CD performance, staging gets medium datasets for pre-production validation. Transactional seeding with rollback ensures data consistency and prevents partial seeding failures that could corrupt the database.

criterion.details: Development: 50 users, 20 files, 100 SMS logs Test: 5 users, 3 files, 10 SMS logs Staging: 25 users, 10 files, 50 SMS logs
criterion.test-strategy: Verify data counts per environment using NODE_ENV=test, NODE_ENV=development, NODE_ENV=staging

criterion.details: All 5 seeders wrapped in prisma.$transaction() in seed.ts main() function
criterion.test-strategy: Review seed.ts source code to confirm $transaction usage

criterion.details: Failed transaction automatically rolls back without leaving partial data
criterion.test-strategy: Inject seeder failure and verify database remains empty

criterion.description: Data integrity across all environment volumes
criterion.details: Foreign key constraints maintained (users→roles, files→users, sms→users)
criterion.test-strategy: Query database to verify all relationships are valid

criterion.details: NODE_ENV=test must complete full seeding in under 3 seconds for CI/CD speed
criterion.test-strategy: Time seed execution with console.time() in test environment

criterion.details: NODE_ENV environment variable automatically controls data volume without manual config
criterion.test-strategy: Verify different NODE_ENV values produce different data volumes

criterion.details: No conflicts between dev/test/staging data, idempotent operations throughout
criterion.test-strategy: Run seeding multiple times and verify no duplicate or conflicting data

technical-context.epic-overview: Epic 13 implements comprehensive seeder architecture using Prisma native seeding, factory pattern with Faker.js, and environment-based data management. Builds on Epic 1 (Database Infrastructure), Epic 3 (Users & Permissions), Epic 4 (Files), and Epic 5 (SMS).
technical-context.story-sequence: Story 13-1: Prisma native seeding entry point (prisma/seed.ts) Story 13-2: Model factory pattern with Faker.js integration Story 13-3: Individual module seeders with idempotent operations Story 13-4: Package.json scripts and Prisma integration Story 13-5: Environment-specific data & transactional seeding (CURRENT) Story 13-6: Hash password utility & test data management Story 13-7: CI/CD integration automation Story 13-8: Testing infrastructure for seeders
technical-context.current-state: Stories 13-1 through 13-4 are complete. The following infrastructure exists: - prisma/seed.ts: Main entry point with basic transaction structure - prisma/seeders/: 5 individual seeder classes (role, permission, user, file, sms) - prisma/factories/: 4 factory classes for data generation - package.json: npm scripts (seed, seed:test, seed:dev, seed:reset) - Prisma native seeding: Configured via prisma.seed in package.json
technical-context.required-changes: 1. Create prisma/config/environment.config.ts with data volume definitions 2. Update seed.ts to use environment config and add error handling 3. Modify all 5 seeders to read data volumes from environment config 4. Enhance transaction wrapper with proper error handling 5. Implement automatic NODE_ENV detection

depends-on.reason: Uses prisma/seed.ts as entry point and main() function structure
Uses factory pattern (UserFactory, FileFactory, etc.) for data generation
Uses 5 individual seeder classes as transaction participants
Uses npm scripts (seed, seed:test, seed:dev) which trigger seeding with NODE_ENV
Requires PrismaService, PrismaClient, and database entities (User, Role, Permission, File, SMS)
Requires TypeScript strict mode (12.4) and import organization (12.5) for type safety

tasks.task: Create environment.config.ts with NODE_ENV→data volume mapping
Update all seeders to import and use environment config
Implement default fallback to development if NODE_ENV invalid
Add meaningful error messages and rollback notifications
Verify all 5 seeders participate in transaction
Test seeding in all 3 environments (dev, test, staging)
Measure and optimize test environment performance
Validate data integrity across all volumes

architecture.description: Environment config (environment.config.ts) defines data volumes per NODE_ENV. All seeders read from config to determine how many records to create. Transaction wrapper ensures atomicity across all seeders. Automatic environment detection via NODE_ENV variable.

component.name: prisma/config/environment.config.ts
component.description: Environment configuration with data volume definitions per NODE_ENV

component.name: prisma/seed.ts
component.description: Enhanced main() function with environment detection and error handling

component.name: prisma/seeders/*.ts
component.description: Updated to read data volumes from environment config instead of hardcoded values

architecture.data-flow: 1. npm run seed (or prisma migrate dev) triggers seed.ts 2. seed.ts detects NODE_ENV (development|test|staging|production) 3. Loads environment.config.ts and reads volume settings 4. Wraps all 5 seeders in $transaction() 5. Each seeder reads config to determine record count 6. All seeders execute within transaction 7. On success: ✓ completion message 8. On failure: ❌ error + automatic rollback

constraint.rationale: Ensures all-or-nothing seeding, prevents partial data corruption

constraint.description: Test environment must seed in < 3 seconds
constraint.rationale: CI/CD performance requirement for fast build pipelines
Safe to re-run scripts multiple times, prevents conflicts

constraint.description: No breaking changes to existing Stories 13.1-13.4
constraint.rationale: Progressive enhancement, not replacement of existing infrastructure

interface.name: IEnvironmentConfig
interface.description: Configuration interface for environment-specific data volumes

properties.property: Number of SMS logs to seed

level.name: Unit
level.description: Test environment config generation and environment detection

cases.case: environment.config.ts exports correct volumes per environment
getEnvironment() returns correct value for different NODE_ENV

level.name: Integration
level.description: Test full seeding in different environments

cases.case: Transaction rollback on failure leaves database empty
Re-running seed produces no duplicate data (idempotent)

level.name: E2E
level.description: Test application functionality with seeded data

cases.case: Login with seeded admin user in test environment
API calls with seeded permissions work correctly
File upload references valid seeded file metadata

code-quality.description: All new code must pass TypeScript strict compilation
Follow 8-category import pattern (libraries, DTOs, services, etc.)
All code formatted and linted without errors

risk.description: Large dataset seeding in development may timeout
risk.mitigation: Environment-based volume limits, chunked processing if needed

risk.description: Foreign key constraint failures if seeding order incorrect
Performance regression in test environment affects CI/CD
risk.mitigation: Monitor seed time, optimize with createMany() for bulk inserts

reference.description: NPM scripts and Prisma integration foundation

file.description: Environment configuration with data volume definitions
Enhanced main() with environment detection and error handling
Update to read from environment config
Update to read from environment config
Update to read from environment config
Update to read from environment config
Update to read from environment config

check-item.description: Environment config file created with correct volumes
check-item.verification: Check prisma/config/environment.config.ts exists and has development/test/staging volumes

check-item.description: All seeders read from environment config
check-item.verification: Verify import and usage of ENVIRONMENT_CONFIG in all 5 seeder files

check-item.description: Transaction wrapper in seed.ts has error handling
check-item.verification: Check seed.ts main() function has try-catch around $transaction
Run `NODE_ENV=test npm run seed` and measure time
Test with NODE_ENV=test, NODE_ENV=development, and unset NODE_ENV
Query database to verify all users have roles, all files have users, etc.
Simulate seeder failure and verify no partial data in database
Run seeding twice and verify no duplicate data
Verify AC1-AC7 are all implemented and tested

question.text: Should production environment seed any data?
question.context: Currently planned as 0 users, 0 files, 0 SMS
question.decision-needed: Confirm production seeding should be disabled

question.text: Should seed isolation use domain-based multi-tenancy?
question.context: Current pattern uses single domain, but architecture supports multi-tenancy
question.decision-needed: Decide if domain isolation is needed for environment separation

question.text: Should we add performance monitoring/alerting for seeding?
question.context: Test environment performance is critical for CI/CD
question.decision-needed: Determine if seeding performance should be monitored beyond basic timing

story-completion-criteria.criterion: All 7 acceptance criteria implemented and tested
Environment config created and used by all seeders
Transaction wrapper with error handling in seed.ts
Data integrity validated across all environments
Story ready for Senior Developer Review
